summary: Matched the Bunny Hood item file itrabbitc by replacing the 531-line assembly file with C, switching build/config entries from asm/nonmatching to src/matching, and tightening related item/fighter prototypes around Item_GObj and ftCo_GObj. The PR also cleaned up local symbol metadata for itrabbitc constants/strings and gave the Light Gun attribute field a semantic name. Human review evidence is minimal: the only visible comment was "Nice job!"
what_changed: Deleted asm/melee/it/items/itrabbitc.s and implemented the corresponding functions in src/melee/it/items/itrabbitc.c.
Marked melee/it/items/itrabbitc.c as Matching in configure.py and changed obj_files.mk to build src/melee/it/items/itrabbitc.c.o instead of asm/melee/it/items/itrabbitc.s.o.
Added the itrabbitc ItemStateTable it_803F6270 in C with five state entries and static callback functions for internal state handlers.

Implemented Bunny Hood item behavior wrappers including shield bounce, spawn setup with SpawnItem, animation state changes through Item_80268E5C, JObj translation updates, fall-speed handling through ItemAttr, and callbacks into item collision/transition helpers.
Removed many public UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations from itrabbitc.h by making internal callbacks static in itrabbitc.c, leaving only externally used APIs exposed.

Changed several fighter/item interfaces from generic HSD_GObj* to more precise aliases such as Item_GObj*, ftCo_GObj*, and Fighter_GObj* in ftpickupitem.c, ftcommon, ftlib, item.c/item.h, and pl_0371.h.
Adjusted config/GALE01/splits.txt sdata range for itrabbitc from end 0x804D52F0 to 0x804D52ED.

Changed itrabbitc constants and assert strings in symbols.txt from global it_804D52E0/it_804D52E8 and it_804DCB58/it_804DCB5C names to local @224/@225 and @183/@184 symbols with string/float metadata.
Renamed ItLGunAttr.x0 to ItLGunAttr.max_ammo and updated itlgun.c to use the semantic field name.
Added a clarifying comment to ftCommon motion vars: itemget.x0 is likely whether itemget action is heavy type.
smart_moves: Kept itrabbitc state callbacks static instead of exporting every matched function, reducing header noise and preserving a cleaner public API.
Used typed GObj aliases across adjacent fighter/item call sites, which likely helped match signatures while documenting object ownership better than raw HSD_GObj*.
Replaced the assembly JObj translation/dirty-flag sequence with higher-level HSD_JObjGetTranslation and HSD_JObjSetTranslate calls, avoiding manual replication of matrix-dirty bit logic in source.
Initialized SpawnItem fields explicitly for it_80294DC0, including kind It_Kind_RabbitC, duplicated prev_pos/pos, facing_dir -1.0f, zero velocity/damage/parent fields, and x44_flag.bits.b0.
Moved address-named float/string constants to local @ symbols in symbols.txt once the C file matched, avoiding unnecessary global names for compiler-emitted literals.
Updated build metadata, splits, symbols, and object lists together with the source match, preventing stale asm/object configuration after deleting the assembly file.
Improved a neighboring item attribute name, ItLGunAttr.max_ammo, where the diff had direct evidence from itlgun initialization.
decomp_lessons: When matching an item file, expect nearby fighter pickup and item helper prototypes to need stronger types; mismatched generic HSD_GObj* signatures can block or obscure a clean match.
Internal item state callbacks should be kept local/static when only the state table or same C file uses them; do not preserve UNK_RET declarations in headers without evidence of external use.
For HSD_JObj position mutations, try HSD_JObjGetTranslation plus HSD_JObjSetTranslate before hand-writing struct offset stores and dirty-matrix logic.
SpawnItem stack setup can often be represented as explicit field assignments; ordering and padding may still require PAD_STACK markers for matching.
Once assembly is removed, remember to update configure.py, obj_files.mk, splits.txt, and symbols.txt in the same PR.
Local strings/floats used only by a newly matched file should generally be represented as local @ symbols rather than exported it_ address names.
Small semantic struct-field renames in shared headers can be worthwhile if direct call-site evidence exists, as with Light Gun max_ammo.
naming_conventions: Use Item_GObj* for item object parameters and returns instead of generic HSD_GObj* when the function operates on items.
Use ftCo_GObj* or Fighter_GObj* for fighter object parameters when the function is fighter-specific.
Keep unknown address-style function names such as it_80294DA0 and it_80295138 when no better semantic name is evidenced.
Prefer semantic attribute field names when evidence is direct; ItLGunAttr.x0 became max_ammo.
Use local @NNN symbols for local literals in symbols.txt, with data:string or data:float metadata, rather than global it_ address labels.
Expose only externally used itrabbitc functions in itrabbitc.h; keep state-table callbacks static in itrabbitc.c.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted the entire itrabbitc text range from assembly to C and deleted the corresponding .s file.
Used PAD_STACK markers in functions where stack layout needed preservation, including it_80294E78, it_80294EB0, it_802950D4, it_8029524C, it_80295298, and a local block in ftpickupitem_800942A0.
Matched repeated simple callbacks as small bool false-return functions or empty void functions with typed unused parameters.
Represented state changes with Item_80268E5C(item_gobj, state, ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE) or the literal update value 6 where evidenced.
Used callback-passing helpers such as it_8026D62C(item_gobj, it_802951C0), it_8026E15C(item_gobj, it_80295138), and it_8026E8C4(item_gobj, it_80295138, it_802951C0).
Updated split sdata end address and local symbol sizes to account for the C compiler's emitted local strings/floats.
Changed the object build route from asm/melee/it/items/itrabbitc.s.o to src/melee/it/items/itrabbitc.c.o only after the file was marked Matching.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.interpretation: Positive review only; no concrete requested changes or rationale were present in the provided slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename itrabbitc address-style functions to semantic Bunny Hood names.
Search for more HSD_GObj* to Item_GObj*/ftCo_GObj* prototype cleanup around item pickup and held item flows.
Check whether Item_8026AB54 in item.h was later updated from HSD_GObj* to Item_GObj* to match the implementation changed in this PR.
Look for other item decomp PRs using HSD_JObjGetTranslation/HSD_JObjSetTranslate to replace manual JObj translation assembly.
Search for additional ItLGunAttr field renames beyond max_ammo.
